straighten_front_leg.cue: Fully straighten your front leg at the top of the jump.
straighten_front_leg.why: A fully extended knee completes the line of the leap; a soft knee reads as unfinished even when height is good.
straighten_front_leg.drill: Lying leg lifts: extend through the knee and hold 3 s, focusing on engaging the quad to pull the kneecap up.

straighten_back_leg.cue: Stretch your back leg long behind you.
straighten_back_leg.why: The back leg finishes the arc of a jeté; energy should reach through the toes.
straighten_back_leg.drill: Standing arabesque press-backs at the barre, 10 slow reps each side.

point_feet.cue: Point through the ball of the foot the instant you leave the floor.
point_feet.why: Pointing extends your line and shows control; flexed feet break the silhouette. The push 'through demi-pointe' also adds height.
point_feet.drill: Theraband points: 3×15 slow reps through demi-pointe to full point each foot.

bigger_split.cue: Brush the front leg higher and drive the back leg behind you.
bigger_split.why: Split angle at the apex is what makes a grand jeté photograph — it comes from an aggressive brush (grand battement) plus hip flexibility.
bigger_split.drill: Grand battements front and back, then supported split holds 30 s each side.

lift_chest.cue: Keep your chest lifted at the top — don't pitch forward.
lift_chest.why: An upright torso keeps the jump looking effortless and protects your landing alignment.
lift_chest.drill: Sautés holding a high fifth-position arm focus, eyes forward.

land_through_plie.cue: Land toe-ball-heel and melt into a deeper plié.
land_through_plie.why: Plié is your shock absorber — a stiff landing sends force into knees and shins and kills the flow into the next step. This is the #1 safety correction.
land_through_plie.drill: Slow landing drills: small sautés focusing only on silent, sequential landings.

snap_to_passe.cue: Snap the working foot to the side of the supporting knee and keep it glued there.
snap_to_passe.why: A high, fixed passé/retiré centers your mass over the supporting leg so the turn stays balanced.
snap_to_passe.drill: Relevé-passé balances, 8 × 10 s holds without turning.

press_releve.cue: Press higher onto relevé and stay there for the whole turn.
press_releve.why: A tall relevé reduces friction with the floor and stacks your weight over the ball of the foot.
press_releve.drill: Slow relevé rises at the barre, 3×10, pausing 2 s at the top.

stack_alignment.cue: Stack shoulders over hips over your supporting foot.
stack_alignment.why: Tilting off the vertical axis is the most common reason a pirouette falls out — rotation can't be sustained off-axis.
stack_alignment.drill: Quarter- and half-turn pirouettes focusing only on finishing in perfect balance, before attempting full rotations.

maintain_seconde_height.cue: Keep your working leg at hip height — don't let it drop as you turn.
maintain_seconde_height.why: The à la seconde position must be maintained throughout the turn; a dropping leg shifts your center of mass sideways and collapses the line of the turn.
maintain_seconde_height.drill: Standing à la seconde holds at the barre: lift the working leg to 90° and hold for 5 s, focusing on the hip flexor maintaining height. 10 reps each side.

extend_seconde_leg.cue: Fully straighten your working leg in à la seconde — knee and foot completely extended.
extend_seconde_leg.why: A bent working knee shortens the line and makes the à la seconde position look incomplete. The leg should reach away from you through a pointed toe.
extend_seconde_leg.drill: Slow développé à la seconde at the barre: unfold the leg with control, finishing with a straight knee and fully pointed foot. 10 reps each side.

lift_arabesque_leg.cue: Press your arabesque leg higher — imagine your heel is being pulled toward the ceiling.
lift_arabesque_leg.why: Height in arabesque comes from active hip extension and hamstring flexibility; a low leg signals unexploited range and weakens the line.
lift_arabesque_leg.drill: Barre arabesque lifts: press the leg up slowly, pause at maximum height for 5 s. 10 reps each side.

straighten_standing_leg.cue: Fully straighten your standing leg — pull up through the quad so the knee locks.
straighten_standing_leg.why: A bent standing leg drops your centre and makes the arabesque look unstable. A straight leg creates the clean vertical line the form requires.
straighten_standing_leg.drill: Single-leg relevé holds (flat foot): fully extend the knee before lifting onto demi-pointe, hold 3 s. 3 × 10 each side.

hold_developpe.cue: Hold the développé — reach the leg to full height and sustain it.
hold_developpe.why: A développé is not a kick; the unfolding motion must arrive at a sustained position that demonstrates control, strength, and flexibility.
hold_developpe.drill: Slow développé to full height at the barre, hold 5 s, lower with control. 10 reps each side. Add a 2-s relevé hold mid-extension as a progression.

kick_higher.cue: Drive your leg higher at the top of the battement — brush it fast and reach for maximum height.
kick_higher.why: Grand battement height comes from a fast, energized brush and hip flexibility; a lazy brush produces a low, unimpressive battement.
kick_higher.drill: Battement tendu combinations: 16 slow tendus → 8 dégagés → 4 battements per direction. Build speed progressively.

deepen_plie.cue: Sink deeper into your plié — let your heels descend and your weight drop through your legs.
deepen_plie.why: Full plié depth demonstrates flexibility and control; a shallow plié signals tension in the calves or inner thighs.
deepen_plie.drill: Slow grand plié series (5 counts down, 5 counts up) in all positions, focusing on sequential heel-ankle-knee release.

track_knees_toes.cue: Push your knees directly over your toes as you plié — do not let them roll in.
track_knees_toes.why: Knees tracking over toes maintains safe alignment and distributes load evenly across the knee joint.
track_knees_toes.drill: Plié with a resistance band just above the knees: press outward against the band throughout the descent.

hold_releve.cue: Hold the relevé without wobbling — find one still point and breathe.
hold_releve.why: Relevé balance trains proprioception and single-leg stability that underpins pirouettes and all supported turns.
hold_releve.drill: Relevé holds: rise to demi-pointe, hold 5 s × 5 reps each foot. Progress to eyes closed.

travel_straight.cue: Travel in a perfectly straight line — pick a spot and drive toward it.
travel_straight.why: Drifting in chaîné turns wastes momentum and looks uncontrolled. Straight-line travel shows precision.
travel_straight.drill: Tape a straight line on the floor; practice chaînés keeping both feet on the tape.

close_fifth_apex.cue: Squeeze your feet together at the top of the jump — land in a tight fifth.
close_fifth_apex.why: For changements and assemblés, the feet must visibly join at the peak; sloppy footwork undercuts the whole phrase.
close_fifth_apex.drill: Small sautés in fifth: focus on a crisp close at the top before landing. 3 × 16.

extend_front_leg_timing.cue: Extend your front leg at the top of the jump, not before — let it unfold at the peak.
extend_front_leg_timing.why: For saut de chat, the leg should unfold at the apex for maximum visual impact; premature extension loses the développé illusion.
extend_front_leg_timing.drill: Practice the développé-in-the-air slowly: mark the takeoff, reach passé, then extend fully at the imagined peak.

reach_legs_wide.cue: Push your legs as far apart as possible — think splits in the air.
reach_legs_wide.why: Toe touch impact comes entirely from the split angle; every degree of spread matters.
reach_legs_wide.drill: Seated straddle holds (30 s × 5) and standing leg swings (front to side, 20 each side).

whip_working_leg.cue: Whip the working leg out to à la seconde and snap it back to passé on every rotation.
whip_working_leg.why: The fouetté works by the working leg's angular momentum being transferred to the body — a half-hearted whip kills the rotation.
whip_working_leg.drill: Mark fouettés slowly: extend fully to à la seconde (pause), snap back to passé (pause). Speed up over 8 reps.

maintain_tilt_line.cue: Keep the tilt angle consistent — don't let your hips drop or your leg sag.
maintain_tilt_line.why: A tilt's impact is the clean diagonal line from foot to head; any sag in the hip or knee breaks the picture.
maintain_tilt_line.drill: Barre tilts: hold the extreme angle for 3 s, focusing on pressing the hip forward and the leg back simultaneously.

level_the_leap.cue: Lift your back leg so the line is level or oversplit — don't let it drop toward the floor.
level_the_leap.why: A dropped back leg collapses the line of the jump; the back leg should reach at least parallel to the floor for the leap to look balanced and powerful.
level_the_leap.drill: Arabesque press-backs and split holds focusing on lifting the back leg above hip height — practise slow grand battements back with a 2-second pause at the top.
